name: lean-ctx
install_doc: README get-started offers `curl -fsSL https://leanctx.com/install.sh | sh`, brew, `npm install -g lean-ctx-bin`, or `cargo install lean-ctx`. We pin the npm prebuilt binary `lean-ctx-bin@3.8.4` (no host build, matches codegraph approach). The stdio MCP server is the bare `lean-ctx` command with NO args (`lean-ctx help all`: 'lean-ctx  Start MCP server (stdio)'; `lean-ctx serve` is the HTTP variant). `lean-ctx onboard` is deliberately NOT used: it rewrites the host ~/.zshrc with a shell hook (host pollution) and the benchmark injects the MCP config directly anyway.
notes: Claims up to 99% savings — exactly the kind of headline THOL exists to test. Single Rust binary exposing 76 MCP tools (ctx_*) at the default profile; those tool schemas are themselves per-turn overhead the benchmark will surface. MCP server validated via an initialize handshake on this machine (serverInfo lean-ctx 3.8.4, capabilities.tools).
